title: botrychium matricariifolium var. hesperium
intro: Botrychium matricariifolium var. hesperium, also known as Matricaria-leaved Grape Fern, is a rare and lesser-known species of the genus Botrychium, a type of fern found in North America. It is used in traditional herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been reported to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Botrychium matricariifolium var. hesperium are typically made by infusing the plant material in hot water or alcohol to create teas, tinctures, or topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
